route.name: Hidden in Plain Sight
route.region: Santa Monica Mountains / Thousand Oaks / Malibu
route.description: Public GPX Santa Monica Mountains route with huge climbing, singletrack, fire roads, pavement, and constrained legal camping.
route.surfaceMix: Public GPX Santa Monica Mountains route with huge climbing, singletrack, fire roads, pavement, and constrained legal camping.
route.bestSeason: October to June is best; July is only plausible with an unusually cool coastal forecast.

difficultyProfile.surface: Public GPX Santa Monica Mountains route with huge climbing, singletrack, fire roads, pavement, and constrained legal camping.
difficultyProfile.traffic: review paved connectors and trailhead access
difficultyProfile.technical: moderate unless source notes say otherwise

resources.water: Water exists at key developed points, but top-offs must be planned carefully.
resources.food: Resupply in Thousand Oaks for the remainder of the route.
resources.camping: Sycamore Canyon hike/bike and Malibu Creek reservations are key legal anchors; availability must be verified.
resources.toiletsShowers: Use mapped waypoints and land-manager pages; confirm current operation before departure.
resources.bikeShops: Identify nearest shops and exact hours during final trip refresh.
resources.longestGap: Measure from imported GPX and source POIs before ride-ready promotion.
resources.mosquitoes: Seasonal; check recent reports for camp and water-adjacent sections.

tips: Promote only with cool marine-layer forecast and confirmed legal camps.

verification.notes: Public GPX imported and parsed locally. Final legal/resource checks still control ride-ready promotion.

mapQuality.reviewRule: Route line is imported from public GPX and suitable for planning review.

review.decision: reject_rework; public GPX exists but July heat, no exact legal overnight sequence, unresolved bike-legal segment overlay, and fire/smoke/weather gates block ride_ready
blockers: Current July 10-13 screening blocks ride-ready promotion. Keep as planning/reference only.
Hidden in Plain Sight full route is not July ride-ready: 104 miles, 13,846 feet, heavy singletrack, and hot inland climbs exceed a conservative next-weekend bikepacking threshold.
Only a shorter coastal/Sycamore Canyon segment should be reconsidered, and it needs its own GPX, water, camp, and fire checks.
July heat gate blocked by route's October-June season guidance and lack of verified NWS point forecast.
Camp gate blocked because Malibu Creek is unavailable for the first two target nights and Point Mugu reservation status is not confirmed for needed dates.
Fire risk gate blocked by active Los Angeles County fire and Red Flag closure sensitivity.
Water and AQI gates not independently verified for ride dates.
July heat gate fails unless final forecast shows unusually cool conditions.
No complete legal overnight sequence is proven for July 10, July 11, and July 12.
Backbone Trail and multi-jurisdiction trail legality has not been cleared segment-by-segment.
Water points are plausible but not July-current for every critical stop.
Regional fire/smoke and closure risk requires a July 7-10 refresh before any reconsideration.
requiredFixes: Resolve the July-window blocker or choose a different route.
Do not promote the full route for July.
If salvaging, create a shorter coastal/Sycamore Canyon GPX segment.
Confirm exact camp/lodging, water points, trail legality, and fire/weather status for that segment.
Secure legal overnight plan for each target night, with confirmation if relying on Sycamore Canyon hike-bike.
Verify Circle X Ranch and intended water points are operating before promotion.
Recheck NWS, CAL FIRE, AirNow, and Santa Monica Mountains/State Parks conditions no earlier than July 7-9.
Do not promote the full route for July 10-13.
If salvaging, create a shorter coastal/Sycamore-specific route with its own GPX.
Capture dated ReserveCalifornia or lodging availability proof for every night.
Overlay GPX against NPS, State Parks, and local open-space bicycle access maps.
Refresh NWS, CAL FIRE, AirNow, park closures, and water operations on July 7-10.
openQuestions: Do current closures/fire/weather alter the imported GPX?

promotionChecklist.targetWindowNote: The full 104-mile, 13,846-foot route is too hard and exposed for July 10-13 ride-ready promotion.
promotionChecklist.officialGeometryNote: Official/public GPX has been imported and parsed locally.
promotionChecklist.campingPermitsNote: Legal hike/bike or reservation camp must be confirmed for the exact July night.
promotionChecklist.closuresFireWeatherNote: Santa Monica Mountains fire/trail closure, forecast, and heat checks remain required.
promotionChecklist.legalAccessNote: Full route legality and any shortened segment need official trail/open-status checks.
promotionChecklist.waterResupplyNote: Route water points exist, but long/hot inland gaps must be measured and likely avoided in a July segment.
promotionChecklist.finalDecisionNote: Source-backed route remains planning-grade; target-window blockers prevent ride-ready promotion only.

days.name: Hidden in Plain Sight segment 1
days.camp: Sycamore Canyon hike/bike and Malibu Creek reservations are key legal anchors; availability must be verified.

waypoints.name: End of Backbone Loop D (w/Sandstone Peak)
Dont linger here - get to trail
A view of Palo Camado Cyn

logistics: Final ride-ready status requires live date-specific resource and legal checks.
Subagent GPX check: public GPX available from Bikepacking.com.
Subagent segment idea: coastal-only overnight to Sycamore Canyon with early starts, omitting hottest inland climbs and long Backbone traverse.

readinessEvidence.finding: Subagent reported the route is best October through June and that summer hills can be very hot; two-day routing has very large climbing days.
Subagent identified a possible safer coastal/Sycamore Canyon rework, but it would be a new segment needing its own GPX and checks.
Route source says best ridden October through June and summer hills can get very hot; legal camping listed around Sycamore Canyon and Malibu Creek.
Reservation availability NO for July 10 and July 11, YES July 12 and July 13; this does not provide a complete target-window camp sequence.
Active Chloe Fire in Los Angeles County started July 1, 282 acres, 23% containment.
Bikepacking.com exposes a GPX and local GPX/GeoJSON artifacts exist.
No complete exact July 10-13 legal overnight sequence is proven; the 3-4 day option has no formal legal camping near Encinal/Corral.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: ReserveCalifornia and route guide overnight notes

readinessEvidence.finding: NPS says only part of the Backbone Trail allows mountain bikes; the GPX has not been cleared segment-by-segment against NPS/State Parks/local bike-legal maps.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: Santa Monica Mountains Backbone Trail information

readinessEvidence.finding: Route source says October-June is best and summer hills can get very hot; July 10-13 forecast is not available on July 2 and regional fire/smoke requires close-in refresh.
readinessEvidence.sourceName: NWS, CAL FIRE, AirNow, and route seasonal guidance
